all validations and asserts if any fail.\n385\t// This is the terminal method - call it to execute all validations.\n386\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n387\t\tv.t.Helper()\n388\t\n389\t\t// Parse response once for all validations\n390\t\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n391\t\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n392\t\t\treturn\n393\t\t}\n394\t\n395\t\t// Run all validations\n396\t\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n397\t\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n398\t\t\t\texp.failed = true\n399\t\t\t}\n400\t\t}\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t// parse parses the S3 error response.\n404\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n405\t\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n406\t\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n410\t\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n411\t\t}\n412\t\n413\t\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n414\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n415\t\t\treturn err\n416\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\treturn nil\n419\t}\n420\t\n421\t// =============================================================================\n422\t// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n423\t// =============================================================================\n424\t\n425\t// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n426\t// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n427\t//\n428\t// It verifies:\n429\t// - HTTP status code matches expected\n430\t// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n431\t// - Response body is not empty\n432\t// - Error code matches expected\n433\t// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n434\t// - Response starts with XML declaration\n435\tfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n436\t\tt.Helper()\n437\t\n438\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n439\t\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n440\t\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n441\t\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n442\t\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n443\t\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n444\t\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n445\t\t\tAssert()\n446\t}\n447\t\n448\t// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\n449\tfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n450\t\tt.Helper()\n451\t\n452\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n453\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n454\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n455\t\t}\n456\t\n457\t\treturn &s3Err\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t// =============================================================================\n461\t// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n462\t// =============================================================================\n463\t// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n464\t// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n465\t// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n466\t// =============================================================================\n467\t\n468\t// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n469\t//\n470\t// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n471\t// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n472\t// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n473\t//\n474\t// Example:\n475\t//\n476\t//\tValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n477\tfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n478\t\tt.Helper()\n479\t\n480\t\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n481\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n482\t\t}\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n486\t//\n487\t// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n488\t// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n489\t// ValidateContentType is defined in content_type_validation.go with enhanced functionality.\n490\t\n491\t// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n492\t//\n493\t// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n494\t// - A non-empty response body\n495\t// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n496\t// - A non-empty error code\n497\t// - A non-empty error message\n498\t//\n499\t// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n500\t//\n501\t// Example:\n502\t//\n503\t//\tValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\n504\tfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n505\t\tt.Helper()\n506\t\n507\t\t// Check response body is not empty\n508\t\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n509\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n510\t\t}\n511\t\n512\t\t// Parse the error response\n513\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n514\t\n515\t\t// Validate error code is present\n516\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n517\t\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n518\t\t}\n519\t\n520\t\t// Validate error message is present\n521\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n522\t\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n523\t\t}\n524\t}\n525\t\n526\t// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n527\t//\n528\t// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n529\t// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n530\t//\n531\t// Example:\n532\t//\n533\t//\tValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\n534\tfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n535\t\tt.Helper()\n536\t\n537\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n538\t\n539\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n540\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n541\t\t}\n542\t}\n543\t\n544\t// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n545\t//\n546\t// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n547\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n548\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n549\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n550\t//\n551\t// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n552\t//\n553\t// Example:\n554\t//\n555\t//\tValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\n556\tfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n557\t\tt.Helper()\n558\t\n559\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n560\t\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n561\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n562\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n563\t\t}\n564\t\n565\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n566\t\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n567\t\tif methods == \"\" {\n568\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n572\t\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n573\t\tif headers == \"\" {\n574\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n575\t\t}\n576\t}\n577\t\n578\t// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n579\t//\n580\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n581\t// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n582\t//\n583\t// Example:\n584\t//\n585\t//\tValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\n586\tfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n587\t\tt.Helper()\n588\t\n589\t\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n590\t\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n591\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n592\t\t}\n593\t}\n594\t\n595\t// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n596\t//\n597\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n598\t// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n599\t//\n600\t// Example:\n601\t//\n602\t//\tValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n603\tfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n604\t\tt.Helper()\n605\t\n606\t\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n607\t\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n608\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n609\t\t}\n610\t}\n611\t\n612\t// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n613\t//\n614\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n615\t// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n616\t//\n617\t// Example:\n618\t//\n619\t//\tValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n620\tfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n621\t\tt.Helper()\n622\t\n623\t\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n624\t\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n625\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n626\t\t}\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\t// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n630\t//\n631\t// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n632\t// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n633\t//\n634\t// Example:\n635\t//\n636\t//\tValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\n637\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n638\t\tt.Helper()\n639\t\n640\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n641\t\n642\t\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n643\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n644\t\t}\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n648\t//\n649\t// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n650\t// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n651\t//\n652\t// Example:\n653\t//\n654\t//\tValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\n655\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n656\t\tt.Helper()\n657\t\n658\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n659\t\n660\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n661\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n662\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n663\t\t}\n664\t}\n665\t\n666\t// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n667\t//\n668\t// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n669\t// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n670\t//\n671\t// Example:\n672\t//\n673\t//\tValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\n674\tfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n675\t\tt.Helper()\n676\t\n677\t\tbody := w.Body.String()\n678\t\n679\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n680\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n681\t\t}\n682\t}\n683\t\n684\t// =============================================================================\n685\t// ERROR RESPONSE STRUCTURE VALIDATION\n686\t// =============================================================================\n687\t// These helpers provide comprehensive error response structure validation\n688\t// with support for optional field validation and detailed error reporting.\n689\t// =============================================================================\n690\t\n691\t// ErrorStructureValidationOptions configures error structure validation.\n692\ttype ErrorStructureValidationOptions struct {\n693\t\t// RequireCode ensures the error code field is present and non-empty\n694\t\tRequireCode bool\n695\t\t// RequireMessage ensures the error message field is present and non-empty\n696\t\tRequireMessage bool\n697\t\t// ExpectedCode checks that the error code matches the expected value\n698\t\tExpectedCode string\n699\t\t// MinMessageLength ensures the error message meets minimum length\n700\t\tMinMessageLength int\n701\t\t// MessageContains checks that the error message contains specific text\n702\t\tMessageContains string\n703\t\t// CustomFields validates additional custom fields in the error response\n704\t\tCustomFields map[string]string\n705\t}\n706\t\n707\t// DefaultValidationOptions returns default validation options.\n708\tfunc DefaultValidationOptions() ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n709\t\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n710\t\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n711\t\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n712\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n713\t\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n714\t\t}\n715\t}\n716\t\n717\t// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates the structure of an error response.\n718\t//\n719\t// This helper function performs comprehensive validation of error response structure:\n720\t// - Validates response body is not empty\n721\t// - Validates error field exists (Code field)\n722\t// - Validates error message is present and non-empty\n723\t// - Supports optional field validation via options\n724\t//\n725\t// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n726\t// Note: This function does not call t.Error() to allow testing of invalid cases.\n727\t// Use AssertValidErrorResponseStructure() for assertion behavior.\n728\t//\n729\t// Parameters:\n730\t//\n731\t//\tt: Testing instance for context\n732\t//\tbody: Response body as byte array\n733\t//\toptions: Validation options (use DefaultValidationOptions() for defaults)\n734\t//\n735\t// Example:\n736\t//\n737\t//\topts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n738\t//\topts.RequireCode = true\n739\t//\topts.RequireMessage = true\n740\t//\topts.MinMessageLength = 15\n741\t//\tvalid := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody, opts)\n742\tfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n743\t\tt.Helper()\n744\t\n745\t\t// Check response body is not empty\n746\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n747\t\t\treturn false\n748\t\t}\n749\t\n750\t\t// Parse the error response\n751\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n752\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n753\t\t\treturn false\n754\t\t}\n755\t\n756\t\t// Track validation results\n757\t\tallValid := true\n758\t\n759\t\t// Validate error code field exists if required\n760\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n761\t\t\tallValid = false\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\t// Validate error message field exists if required\n765\t\tif options.RequireMessage {\n766\t\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n767\t\t\tif trimmedMsg == \"\" {\n768\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n769\t\t\t}\n770\t\t}\n771\t\n772\t\t// Validate expected code if specified\n773\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n774\t\t\tallValid = false\n775\t\t}\n776\t\n777\t\t// Validate minimum message length if specified\n778\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n779\t\t\t// Use trimmed message for length validation\n780\t\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n781\t\t\tif len(trimmedMsg) < options.MinMessageLength {\n782\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n783\t\t\t}\n784\t\t}\n785\t\n786\t\t// Validate message contains expected text if specified (case-insensitive)\n787\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" {\n788\t\t\tmessageLower := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n789\t\t\tkeywordLower := strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)\n790\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(messageLower, keywordLower) {\n791\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n792\t\t\t}\n793\t\t}\n794\t\n795\t\t// Validate custom fields if specified\n796\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n797\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n798\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n799\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n800\t\t\t}\n801\t\t}\n802\t\n803\t\treturn allValid\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with defaults.\n807\t//\n808\t// This is a simplified version that uses default validation options.\n809\t// It validates:\n810\t// - Response body is not empty\n811\t// - Error code field exists and is non-empty\n812\t// - Error message field exists and is non-empty (min 10 chars)\n813\t//\n814\t// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n815\t//\n816\t// Example:\n817\t//\n818\t//\tvalid := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, responseBody)\n819\tfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n820\t\tt.Helper()\n821\t\treturn ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, DefaultValidationOptions())\n822\t}\n823\t\n824\t// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure validates error structure and asserts if invalid.\n825\t//\n826\t// This helper validates error response structure and fails the test if validation fails.\n827\t// Use this when you want test execution to stop on validation failure.\n828\t// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n829\t//\n830\t// Example:\n831\t//\n832\t//\tAssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\n833\tfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte) {\n834\t\tt.Helper()\n835\t\n836\t\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body) {\n837\t\t\t// Provide detailed error message\n838\t\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n839\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n840\t\t\t}\n841\t\n842\t\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n843\t\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n844\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n845\t\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n848\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n849\t\t\t}\n850\t\n851\t\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n852\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n853\t\t\t}\n854\t\n855\t\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < 10 {\n856\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least 10): %s\",\n857\t\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), s3Err.Message)\n858\t\t\t}\n859\t\n860\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n861\t\t}\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions validates with custom options and asserts.\n865\t//\n866\t// This helper validates error response structure with custom options and fails the test\n867\t// if validation fails. Use this when you need specific validation requirements.\n868\t// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n869\t//\n870\t// Example:\n871\t//\n872\t//\topts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n873\t//\topts.MinMessageLength = 20\n874\t//\topts.MessageContains = \"authentication\"\n875\t//\tAssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, responseBody, opts)\n876\tfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n877\t\tt.Helper()\n878\t\n879\t\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options) {\n880\t\t\t// Provide detailed error message based on options\n881\t\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n882\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n883\t\t\t}\n884\t\n885\t\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n886\t\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n887\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n888\t\t\t}\n889\t\n890\t\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n891\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n892\t\t\t}\n893\t\n894\t\t\tif options.RequireMessage && s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n895\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n896\t\t\t}\n897\t\n898\t\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n899\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected code '%s', got '%s'\",\n900\t\t\t\t\toptions.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n901\t\t\t}\n902\t\n903\t\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(s3Err.Message) < options.MinMessageLength {\n904\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n905\t\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), options.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n906\t\t\t}\n907\t\n908\t\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, options.MessageContains) {\n909\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n910\t\t\t\t\toptions.MessageContains, s3Err.Message)\n911\t\t\t}\n912\t\n913\t\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n914\t\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n915\t\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n916\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected field '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\",\n917\t\t\t\t\t\tfield, expectedValue, actualValue)\n918\t\t\t\t}\n919\t\t\t}\n920\t\n921\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n922\t\t}\n923\t}\n924\t\n925\t// getXMLField extracts a field value from XML response body.\n926\t// This is a helper for validating custom fields in error responses.\n927\tfunc getXMLField(body []byte, field string) string {\n928\t\t// Simple XML field extraction using string search\n929\t\t// This is basic but works for common S3 error response structures\n930\t\topenTag := \"<\" + field + \">\"\n931\t\tcloseTag := \"</\" + field + \">\"\n932\t\n933\t\tbodyStr := string(body)\n934\t\topenIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr, openTag)\n935\t\tif openIdx == -1 {\n936\t\t\treturn \"\"\n937\t\t}\n938\t\n939\t\tcloseIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr[openIdx:], closeTag)\n940\t\tif closeIdx == -1 {\n941\t\t\treturn \"\"\n942\t\t}\n943\t\n944\t\treturn strings.TrimSpace(bodyStr[openIdx+len(openTag) : openIdx+closeIdx])\n945\t}\n946\t\n947\t// =============================================================================\n948\t// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n949\t// =============================================================================\n950\t\n951\t// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n952\t//\n953\t// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n954\t// It supports:\n955\t// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n956\t// - Optional request body\n957\t// - Custom headers\n958\t// - Test server URL format\n959\tfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n960\t\tt.Helper()\n961\t\n962\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n963\t\n964\t\t// Set default headers for test requests\n965\t\tif headers == nil {\n966\t\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n967\t\t}\n968\t\n969\t\t// Set custom headers\n970\t\tfor key, value := range headers {\n971\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n972\t\t}\n973\t\n974\t\treturn req\n975\t}\n976\t\n977\t// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n978\t//\n979\t// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n980\t// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\n981\tfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n982\t\tt.Helper()\n983\t\n984\t\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n985\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n986\t}\n987\t\n988\t// =============================================================================\n989\t// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n990\t// =============================================================================\n991\t\n992\t// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\n993\ttype ErrorScenario struct {\n994\t\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n995\t\tName string\n996\t\n997\t\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n998\t\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n999\t\n1000\t\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n1001\t\tExpectedCode string\n1002\t\n1003\t\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n1004\t\tExpectedStatus int\n1005\t\n1006\t\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n1007\t\tExpectedKeywords []string\n1008\t\n1009\t\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n1010\t\tMinMessageLength int\n1011\t\n1012\t\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n1013\t\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n1014\t\n1015\t\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n1016\t\tDescription string\n1017\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\n1020\ttype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n1021\t\tScenarioName string\n1022\t\tStatusCode   int\n1023\t\tErrorCode    string\n1024\t\tMessage      string\n1025\t\tDuration     time.Duration\n1026\t\tPassed       bool\n1027\t}\n1028\t\n1029\t// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\n1030\tfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n1031\t\tt.Helper()\n1032\t\n1033\t\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n1034\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1035\t\n1036\t\t// Measure response time\n1037\t\tstart := time.Now()\n1038\t\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n1039\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n1040\t\n1041\t\t// Parse response\n1042\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n1043\t\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n1044\t\n1045\t\t// Build result\n1046\t\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n1047\t\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n1048\t\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n1049\t\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n1050\t\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n1051\t\t\tDuration:     duration,\n1052\t\t}\n1053\t\n1054\t\t// Validate result\n1055\t\tresult.Passed = true\n1056\t\n1057\t\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n1058\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n1059\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1060\t\t}\n1061\t\n1062\t\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n1063\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n1064\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1065\t\t}\n1066\t\n1067\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < scenario.MinMessageLength {\n1068\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n1093\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1094\t\t}\n1095\t\n1096\t\treturn result\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// {\n1150\t\tsuite.t.Helper()\n1151\t\n1152\t\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n1153\t\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n1154\t\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n1165\t\tsuite.t.Helper()\n1166\t\n1167\t\tif len(suite.results) == 0 {\n1168\t\t\tsuite.t.Log(\"No results to report\")\n1169\t\t\treturn\n1170\t\t}\n1171\t\n1172\t\tvar total, max, min TESTACCESSKEY/TESTSECRETKEY...\n// - Full access (no ACL restrictions)\n//\n// Returns a TestServerFixture ready for error testing.\nfunc NewTestServer(t *testing.T) *TestServerFixture {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tcredentials := map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\"TESTACCESSKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"TESTACCESSKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\",\n\t\t\tACLs:      nil, // Full access\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"RESTRICTEDKEY\": {\n\t\t\tAccessKey: \"RESTRICTEDKEY\",\n\t\t\tSecretKey: \"HTTPStatusCode\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tif ev.response.Code != code {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status %d, got %d\", code, ev.response.Code)\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// ContentType verifies the Content-Type header.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// HasCode verifies the S3 error code.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasCode(code string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"HasCode\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); HasCORSHeaders verifies that CORS headers are present on the error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasCORSHeaders() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"HasCORSHeaders\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\torigin := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSOrigin verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSOrigin(origin string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSOrigin\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\t\tif actual != origin {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", origin, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSMethods verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSMethods(methods string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSMethods\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\t\tif actual != methods {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", methods, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSHeaders verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSHeaders(headers string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSHeaders\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\t\tif actual != headers {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS headers '%s', got '%s'\", headers, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// Assert runs all validations and asserts if any fail.\n// This is the terminal method - call it to execute all validations.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n\tv.t.Helper()\n\n\t// Parse response once for all validations\n\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Run all validations\n\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n\t\t\texp.failed = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// parse parses the S3 error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n\t}\n\n\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n\t}\n\n\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n//\n// It verifies:\n// - HTTP status code matches expected\n// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code matches expected\n// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n// - Response starts with XML declaration\nfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n\t\tAssert()\n}\n\n// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\nfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &s3Err\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n// ValidateContentType is defined in content_type_validation.go with enhanced functionality.\n\n// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n// - A non-empty response body\n// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n// - A non-empty error code\n// - A non-empty error message\n//\n// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\nfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\t// Validate error code is present\n\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message is present\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n//\n// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\nfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif methods == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif headers == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\nfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\nfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\nfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\nfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody := w.Body.String()\n\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR RESPONSE STRUCTURE VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide comprehensive error response structure validation\n// with support for optional field validation and detailed error reporting.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorStructureValidationOptions configures error structure validation.\ntype ErrorStructureValidationOptions struct {\n\t// RequireCode ensures the error code field is present and non-empty\n\tRequireCode bool\n\t// RequireMessage ensures the error message field is present and non-empty\n\tRequireMessage bool\n\t// ExpectedCode checks that the error code matches the expected value\n\tExpectedCode string\n\t// MinMessageLength ensures the error message meets minimum length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\t// MessageContains checks that the error message contains specific text\n\tMessageContains string\n\t// CustomFields validates additional custom fields in the error response\n\tCustomFields map[string]string\n}\n\n// DefaultValidationOptions returns default validation options.\nfunc DefaultValidationOptions() ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates the structure of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function performs comprehensive validation of error response structure:\n// - Validates response body is not empty\n// - Validates error field exists (Code field)\n// - Validates error message is present and non-empty\n// - Supports optional field validation via options\n//\n// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n// Note: This function does not call t.Error() to allow testing of invalid cases.\n// Use AssertValidErrorResponseStructure() for assertion behavior.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//\n//\tt: Testing instance for context\n//\tbody: Response body as byte array\n//\toptions: Validation options (use DefaultValidationOptions() for defaults)\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\topts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n//\topts.RequireCode = true\n//\topts.RequireMessage = true\n//\topts.MinMessageLength = 15\n//\tvalid := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody, opts)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Track validation results\n\tallValid := true\n\n\t// Validate error code field exists if required\n\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tallValid = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message field exists if required\n\tif options.RequireMessage {\n\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tif trimmedMsg == \"\" {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate expected code if specified\n\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tallValid = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate minimum message length if specified\n\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n\t\t// Use trimmed message for length validation\n\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tif len(trimmedMsg) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate message contains expected text if specified (case-insensitive)\n\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" {\n\t\tmessageLower := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tkeywordLower := strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(messageLower, keywordLower) {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate custom fields if specified\n\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn allValid\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with defaults.\n//\n// This is a simplified version that uses default validation options.\n// It validates:\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code field exists and is non-empty\n// - Error message field exists and is non-empty (min 10 chars)\n//\n// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tvalid := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, responseBody)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\treturn ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, DefaultValidationOptions())\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure validates error structure and asserts if invalid.\n//\n// This helper validates error response structure and fails the test if validation fails.\n// Use this when you want test execution to stop on validation failure.\n// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tAssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body) {\n\t\t// Provide detailed error message\n\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least 10): %s\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions validates with custom options and asserts.\n//\n// This helper validates error response structure with custom options and fails the test\n// if validation fails. Use this when you need specific validation requirements.\n// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\topts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n//\topts.MinMessageLength = 20\n//\topts.MessageContains = \"authentication\"\n//\tAssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, responseBody, opts)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options) {\n\t\t// Provide detailed error message based on options\n\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.RequireMessage && s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected code '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\toptions.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(s3Err.Message) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), options.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, options.MessageContains) {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\toptions.MessageContains, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected field '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfield, expectedValue, actualValue)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// getXMLField extracts a field value from XML response body.\n// This is a helper for validating custom fields in error responses.\nfunc getXMLField(body []byte, field string) string {\n\t// Simple XML field extraction using string search\n\t// This is basic but works for common S3 error response structures\n\topenTag := \"<\" + field + \">\"\n\tcloseTag := \"</\" + field + \">\"\n\n\tbodyStr := string(body)\n\topenIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr, openTag)\n\tif openIdx == -1 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tcloseIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr[openIdx:], closeTag)\n\tif closeIdx == -1 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(bodyStr[openIdx+len(openTag) : openIdx+closeIdx])\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n//\n// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n// It supports:\n// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n// - Optional request body\n// - Custom headers\n// - Test server URL format\nfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n\n\t// Set default headers for test requests\n\tif headers == nil {\n\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set custom headers\n\tfor key, value := range headers {\n\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n//\n// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\nfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\ntype ErrorScenario struct {\n\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n\tName string\n\n\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int\n\n\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedKeywords []string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n\tDescription string\n}\n\n// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\ntype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n\tScenarioName string\n\tStatusCode   int\n\tErrorCode    string\n\tMessage      string\n\tDuration     time.Duration\n\tPassed       bool\n}\n\n// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\nfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t// Measure response time\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\n\t// Parse response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n\n\t// Build result\n\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n\t\tDuration:     duration,\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate result\n\tresult.Passed = true\n\n\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < scenario.MinMessageLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tscenario.Name, len(s3Err.Message), scenario.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check keywords if specified\n\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedKeywords, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check response time if specified\n\tif scenario.MaxResponseTime > 0 && duration > scenario.MaxResponseTime {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", scenario.Name, duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASE TEST STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenarioSuite provides a test suite structure for running error scenarios.\n//\n// This structure helps organize related error tests and provides\n// consistent reporting and validation across test types.\ntype ErrorScenarioSuite struct {\n\tt         *testing.T\n\tname      string\n\tfixture   *TestServerFixture\n\tscenarios []ErrorScenario\n\tresults   []*ErrorScenarioResult\n}\n\n// NewErrorScenarioSuite creates a new error scenario suite.\nfunc NewErrorScenarioSuite(t *testing.T, name string) *ErrorScenarioSuite {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn &ErrorScenarioSuite{\n\t\tt:       t,\n\t\tname:    name,\n\t\tfixture: NewTestServer(t),\n\t\tresults: make([]*ErrorScenarioResult, 0),\n\t}\n}\n\n// AddScenario adds a test scenario to the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorScenarioSuite) AddScenario(scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioSuite {\n\tsuite.scenarios = append(suite.scenarios, scenario)\n\treturn suite\n}\n\n// Run executes all scenarios in the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorScenarioSuite) Run() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// RunSequentially executes all scenarios sequentially (not in parallel).\n// Use this when scenarios have dependencies or share state.\nfunc (suite *ErrorScenarioSuite) RunSequentially() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Parallel() // Disable parallelism\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// ReportStatistics logs performance statistics for the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorScenarioSuite) ReportStatistics() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tif len(suite.results) == 0 {\n\t\tsuite.t.Log(\"No results to report\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar total, max, min time.Duration\n\tmin = 1 * time.Second // Initialize to high value\n\tpassed := 0\n\tfailed := 0\n\n\tfor _, result := range suite.results {\n\t\ttotal += result.Duration\n\t\tif result.Duration > max {\n\t\t\tmax = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Duration < min {\n\t\t\tmin = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Passed {\n\t\t\tpassed++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfailed++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tavg := total / time.Duration(len(suite.results))\n\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"Error Test Suite: %s\", suite.name)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Total scenarios: %d\", len(suite.results))\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Passed: %d, Failed: %d\", passed, failed)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Average response time: %v\", avg)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Min response time: %v\", min)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Max response time: %v\", max)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// min returns the minimum of two integers.\nfunc min(a, b int) int {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// MeasureRequestTime measures the time to execute a request.\n// Returns the duration and response recorder.\nfunc MeasureRequestTime(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) (time.Duration, *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\treturn duration, w\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DOCUMENTATION AND EXAMPLES\n// =============================================================================\n\n/*\nExample Usage:\n\nBasic error response validation:\n\n    func TestMyError(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n        VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\", 404)\n    }\n\nAdvanced validation with fluent API:\n\n    func TestMyErrorAdvanced(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"POST\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil, nil)\n        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n        duration, _ := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n\n        VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n            HTTPStatusCode(400).\n            ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n            HasCode(\"InvalidRequest\").\n            MessageContainsAny(\"post\", \"unsupported\", \"operation\").\n            ResponseTime(100 * time.Millisecond).\n            Assert()\n    }\n\nCORS header validation:\n\n    func TestErrorCORSHeaders(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n        VerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n            HTTPStatusCode(404).\n            HasCORSHeaders().\n }\n\nUsing test suites:\n\n    func TestMyErrorSuite(t *testing.T) {\n        suite := NewErrorScenarioSuite(t, \"My Error Scenarios\").\n            AddScenario(ErrorScenario{\n                Name: \"Scenario 1\",\n                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                    return CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key1\", nil, nil)\n                },\n                ExpectedCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n                ExpectedStatus: 404,\n                MinMessageLength: 15,\n                MaxResponseTime: 100 * time.Millisecond,\n            }).\n            AddScenario(ErrorScenario{\n                Name: \"Scenario 2\",\n                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                    return CreateTestRequest(t, \"POST\",        suite.Run()\n        suite.ReportStatistics()\n    }\n\nUsing error scenarios directly:\n\n    func TestMyScenario(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        scenario := ErrorScenario{\n            Name: \"My custom scenario\",\n            SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                return CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test\", nil, nil)\n            },\n            ExpectedCode: \"NoSuchKey\",\n            ExpectedStatus: 404,\n            ExpectedKeywords: []string{\"not found\", \"object\"},\n            MinMessageLength: 15,\n            MaxResponseTime: 100 * time.Millisecond,\n        }\n\n        result := RunErrorScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n\n        if !result.Passed {\n            t.Errorf(\"Scenario failed: %s\", result.Message)\n        }\n    }\n*/\n","numLines":1333,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1333}}}

It extends the infrastructure in error_test_infrastructure.go\n15\t// with organized patterns for common error scenarios.\n16\t//\n17\t// Design Philosophy:\n18\t// - Table-driven tests for consistency\n19\t// - Composable patterns for flexibility\n20\t// - Clear extension points for custom scenarios\n21\t// - Self-documenting test structures\n22\t//\n23\t// Usage:\n24\t//   1. Choose a base pattern that matches your error type\n25\t//   2. Extend the test table with your specific scenarios\n26\t//   3. Use the provided helpers to execute tests\n27\t//   4. Leverage predefined validators for common assertions\n28\t// =============================================================================\n29\t\n30\t// =============================================================================\n31\t// BASE TEST TABLE STRUCTURES\n32\t// =============================================================================\n33\t\n34\t// CommonErrorTestCase is the base structure for all error test cases.\n35\t// This structure captures the common elements across all error types.\n36\ttype CommonErrorTestCase struct {\n37\t\t// Name is the test case name for reporting\n38\t\tName string\n39\t\n40\t\t// Description explains what this test case validates\n41\t\tDescription string\n42\t\n43\t\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n44\t\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n45\t\n46\t\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n47\t\tExpectedStatus int\n48\t\n49\t\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n50\t\tExpectedCode string\n51\t\n52\t\t// ExpectedMessageSubstring is text that should appear in the error message\n53\t\tExpectedMessageSubstring string\n54\t\n55\t\t// ExpectedMessageKeywords are alternative keywords (any match is acceptable)\n56\t\tExpectedMessageKeywords []string\n57\t\n58\t\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length (default: 15)\n59\t\tMinMessageLength int\n60\t\n61\t\t// ValidateResponse provides custom validation beyond the standard checks\n62\t\tValidateResponse func(*testing.T, *httptest.ResponseRecorder)\n63\t\n64\t\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n65\t\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n66\t\n67\t\t// SkipCORSValidation disables CORS header checks (for non-CORS scenarios)\n68\t\tSkipCORSValidation bool\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t// AuthenticationErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for auth-specific errors.\n72\ttype AuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n73\t\tCommonErrorTestCase\n74\t\n75\t\t// AccessKey used in the request (for documentation)\n76\t\tAccessKey string\n77\t\n78\t\t// AuthErrorType categorizes the authentication failure\n79\t\tAuthErrorType string\n80\t\n81\t\t// ExpectedAuthError is the specific error expected from auth verification\n82\t\tExpectedAuthError error\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\t// NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for non-auth errors.\n86\ttype NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase string\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t// CORSErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for CORS-specific testing.\n100\ttype CORSErrorTestCase struct {\n101\t\tCommonErrorTestCase\n102\t\n103\t\t// Origin is the SupportedContentTypes are the types that should be mentioned in the error\n130\t\tSupportedContentTypes []string\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// =============================================================================\n134\t// CORE ERROR TEST DATA STRUCTURES\n135\t// across different test scenarios and types.\n138\t// =============================================================================\n139\t\n140\t// PatternErrorTestCase represents a single error test case with all necessary validation parameters.\n141\t// This is the core structure that defines what to test and how to validate the response.\n142\ttype PatternErrorTestCase struct {\n143\t\t// Name ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n156\t\tExpectedCode string `json:\"expected_code\" validate:\"required\"`\n157\t\n158\t\t// ExpectedMessageSubstring is text that should appear in the error =============================================================================\n605\t// These helpers execute test cases with standardized validation.\n606\t// =============================================================================\n607\t\n608\t// RunAuthenticationTestCase executes a single authentication error test case.\n609\t// It performs standard validation plus authentication-specific checks.\n610\tfunc RunAuthenticationTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt AuthenticationErrorTestCase) {\n611\t\tt.Helper()\n612\t\n613\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n614\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n615\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n616\t\n617\t\t// Standard validation\n618\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n619\t\n620\t\t// Authentication-specific validation\n621\t\tif tt.ExpectedAuthError != nil {\n622\t\t\t// The error response should indicate the auth failure type\n623\t\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n624\t\t\t\tMessageContainsAny(tt.AuthErrorType).\n625\t\t\t\tAssert()\n626\t\t}\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\t// RunNonAuthenticationTestCase executes a single non-authentication error test case.\n630\t// It performs standard validation for non-auth errors.\n631\tfunc RunNonAuthenticationTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase) {\n632\t\tt.Helper()\n633\t\n634\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n635\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n636\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n637\t\n638\t\t// Standard validation\n639\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t// RunCORSTestCase executes a single CORS error test case.\n643\t// It performs standard validation plus CORS-specific checks.\n644\tfunc RunCORSTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt CORSErrorTestCase) {\n645\t\tt.Helper()\n646\t\n647\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n648\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n649\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n650\t\n651\t\t// Standard validation\n652\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n653\t\n654\t\t// CORS-specific validation\n655\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n656\t\t\tCORSOrigin(tt.ExpectedCORSOrigin).\n657\t\t\tCORSMethods(tt.ExpectedCORSMethods).\n658\t\t\tCORSHeaders(tt.ExpectedCORSHeaders).\n659\t\t\tAssert()\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t// RunContentTypeTestCase executes a single content-type error test case.\n663\t// It performs standard validation plus content-type-specific checks.\n664\tfunc RunContentTypeTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt ContentTypeErrorTestCase) {\n665\t\tt.Helper()\n666\t\n667\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n668\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n669\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n670\t\n671\t\t// Standard validation\n672\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n673\t\n674\t\t// Content-type-specific validation\n675\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n676\t\t\tMessageContainsAny(tt.ExpectedContentType).\n677\t\t\tAssert()\n678\t}\n679\t\n680\t// RunCommonErrorValidations performs standard validation for all error types.\n681\t// This is called by the specific test case runners.\n682\tfunc RunCommonErrorValidations(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, tt CommonErrorTestCase, duration time.Duration) {\n683\t\tt.Helper()\n684\t\n685\t\t// Use the fluent validator for comprehensive checks\n686\t\tvalidator := VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration)\n687\t\n688\t\t// Always validate HTTP status\n689\t\tvalidator.HTTPStatusCode(tt.ExpectedStatus)\n690\t\n691\t\t// Always validate Content-Type\n692\t\tvalidator.ContentType(\"application/xml\")\n693\t\n694\t\t// Always validate error code\n695\t\tif tt.ExpectedCode != \"\" {\n696\t\t\tvalidator.HasCode(tt.ExpectedCode)\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Always validate message is present\n700\t\tvalidator.BodyNotEmpty()\n701\t\n702\t\t// Always validate XML declaration\n703\t\tvalidator.HasXMLDeclaration()\n704\t\n705\t\t// Validate message substring or keywords\n706\t\tif tt.ExpectedMessageSubstring != \"\" {\n707\t\t\tvalidator.HasMessage(tt.ExpectedMessageSubstring)\n708\t\t} else if len(tt.ExpectedMessageKeywords) > 0 {\n709\t\t\tvalidator.MessageContainsAny(tt.ExpectedMessageKeywords...)\n710\t\t}\n711\t\n712\t\t// Validate minimum message length\n713\t\tif tt.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n714\t\t\tvalidator.MessageMinLength(tt.MinMessageLength)\n715\t\t} else {\n716\t\t\tvalidator.MessageMinLength(15) // Default minimum\n717\t\t}\n718\t\n719\t\t// Validate response time if specified\n720\t\tif tt.MaxResponseTime > 0 {\n721\t\t\tvalidator.ResponseTime(tt.MaxResponseTime)\n722\t\t}\n723\t\n724\t\t// Validate CORS headers unless skipped\n725\t\tif !tt.SkipCORSValidation {\n726\t\t\tvalidator.HasCORSHeaders()\n727\t\t}\n728\t\n729\t\t// Execute all validations\n730\t\tvalidator.Assert()\n731\t\n732\t\t// Run custom validation if provided\n733\t\tif tt.ValidateResponse != nil {\n734\t\t\ttt.ValidateResponse(t, w)\n735\t\t}\n736\t}\n737\t\n738\t// =============================================================================\n739\t// TABLE-DRIVEN TEST RUNNERS\n740\t// =============================================================================\n741\t// These helpers run entire test tables with organized reporting.\n742\t// =============================================================================\n743\t\n744\t// RunAuthenticationErrorTable executes a table of authentication error tests.\n745\t// Use this for running authentication test suites with consistent reporting.\n746\tfunc RunAuthenticationErrorTable(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tests []AuthenticationErrorTestCase) {\n747\t\tt.Helper()\n748\t\n749\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n750\t\t\tt.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n751\t\t\t\tif tt.Description != \"\" {\n752\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Test: %s\", tt.Description)\n753\t\t\t\t}\n754\t\t\t\tRunAuthenticationTestCase(t, fixture, tt)\n755\t\t\t})\n756\t\t}\n757\t}\n758\t\n759\t// RunNonAuthenticationErrorTable executes a table of non-authentication error tests.\n760\t// Use this for running non-authentication test suites with consistent reporting.\n761\tfunc RunNonAuthenticationErrorTable(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tests []NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase) {\n762\t\tt.Helper()\n763\t\n764\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n765\t\t\tt.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n766\t\t\t\tif tt.Description != \"\" {\n767\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Test: %s\", tt.Description)\n768\t\t\t\t}\n769\t\t\t\tRunNonAuthenticationTestCase(t, fixture, tt)\n770\t\t\t})\n771\t\t}\n772\t}\n773\t\n774\t// RunCORSErrorTable executes a table of CORS error tests.\n775\t// Use this for running CORS test suites with consistent reporting.\n776\tfunc RunCORSErrorTable(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tests []CORSErrorTestCase) {\n777\t\tt.Helper()\n778\t\n779\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n780\t\t\tt.Run(tt.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n781\t\t\t\tif tt.Description != \"\" {\n782\t\t\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Test: %s\", tt.Description)\n783\t\t\t\t}\n784\t\t\t\tRunCORSTestCase(t, fixture, tt)\n785\t\t\t})\n786\t\t}\n787\t}\n788\t\n789\t// =============================================================================\n790\t// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n791\t// =============================================================================\n792\t// These helpers create requests for common error scenarios.\n793\t// =============================================================================\n794\t\n795\t// createMissingAuthHeaderRequest creates a request without auth header.\n796\tfunc createMissingAuthHeaderRequest(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n797\t\tt.Helper()\n798\t\treturn CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n799\t}\n800\t\n801\t// createInvalidKeyRequest creates a request with an invalid access key.\n802\tfunc createInvalidKeyRequest(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n803\t\tt.Helper()\n804\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", \"\", \"INVALIDKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t// createInvalidSignatureRequest creates a request with an invalid signature.\n808\tfunc createInvalidSignatureRequest(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n809\t\tt.Helper()\n810\t\treq := createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n811\t\t// Tamper with signature\n812\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTACCESSKEY/20130524/us-east-005/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=BADSIGNATURE1234567890abcdef\")\n813\t\treturn req\n814\t}\n815\t\n816\t// createMalformedAuthRequest creates a request with a malformed auth header.\n817\tfunc createMalformedAuthRequest(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n818\t\tt.Helper()\n819\t\treq := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", nil, nil)\n820\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"InvalidAuthHeaderFormat\")\n821\t\treturn req\n822\t}\n823\t\n824\t// createMissingDateRequest creates a request without a date header.\n825\tfunc createMissingDateRequest(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n826\t\tt.Helper()\n827\t\treq := createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n828\t\treq.Header.Del(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n829\t\treturn req\n830\t}\n831\t\n832\t// createExpiredRequest creates a request with an expired timestamp.\n833\tfunc createExpiredRequest(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n834\t\tt.Helper()\n835\t\toldTime := time.Now().Add(-20 * time.Minute)\n836\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", &oldTime)\n837\t}\n838\t\n839\t// createNotFoundRequest creates a
