server\n\nimport (\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TEST PATTERNS\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides base test patterns and reusable test table structures\n// for error response testing. It extends the infrastructure in error_test_infrastructure.go\n// with organized patterns for common error scenarios.\n//\n// Design Philosophy:\n// - Table-driven tests for consistency\n// - Composable patterns for flexibility\n// - Clear extension points for custom scenarios\n// - Self-documenting test structures\n//\n// Usage:\n//   1. Choose a base pattern that matches your error type\n//   2. Extend the test table with your specific scenarios\n//   3. Use the provided helpers to execute tests\n//   4. Leverage predefined validators for common assertions\n// =============================================================================\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASE TEST TABLE STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CommonErrorTestCase is the base structure for all error test cases.\n// This structure captures the common elements across all error types.\ntype CommonErrorTestCase struct {\n\t// Name is the test case name for reporting\n\tName string\n\n\t// Description explains what this test case validates\n\tDescription string\n\n\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// ExpectedMessageSubstring is text that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedMessageSubstring string\n\n\t// ExpectedMessageKeywords are alternative keywords (any match is acceptable)\n\tExpectedMessageKeywords []string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length (default: 15)\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\n\t// ValidateResponse provides custom validation beyond the standard checks\n\tValidateResponse func(*testing.T, *httptest.ResponseRecorder)\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// SkipCORSValidation disables CORS header checks (for non-CORS scenarios)\n\tSkipCORSValidation bool\n}\n\n// AuthenticationErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for auth-specific errors.\ntype AuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n\tCommonErrorTestCase\n\n\t// AccessKey used in the request (for documentation)\n\tAccessKey string\n\n\t// AuthErrorType categorizes the authentication failure\n\tAuthErrorType string\n\n\t// ExpectedAuthError is the specific error expected from auth verification\n\tExpectedAuthError error\n}\n\n// NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for non-auth errors.\ntype NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n\tCommonErrorTestCase\n\n\t// ErrorCategory categorizes the non-auth error (e.g., \"NotFound\", \"InvalidRequest\")\n\tErrorCategory string\n\n\t// RequiresAuth indicates whether the request needs valid authentication\n\tRequiresAuth bool\n\n\t// ResourcePath is the path being accessed (for documentation)\n\tResourcePath string\n}\n\n// CORSErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for CORS-specific testing.\ntype CORSErrorTestCase struct {\n\tCommonErrorTestCase\n\n\t// Origin is the CORS origin for the request\n\tOrigin string\n\n\t// ExpectedCORSOrigin is the expected CORS Allow-Origin header value\n\tExpectedCORSOrigin string\n\n\t// ExpectedCORSMethods is the expected CORS Allow-Methods header value\n\tExpectedCORSMethods string\n\n\t// ExpectedCORSHeaders is the expected CORS Allow-Headers header value\n\tExpectedCORSHeaders string\n\n\t// IsPreflight indicates this is a CORS preflight request\n\tIsPreflight bool\n}\n\n// ContentTypeErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for content-type errors.\ntype ContentTypeErrorTestCase struct {\n\tCommonErrorTestCase\n\n\t// RequestContentType is the Content-Type header in the request\n\tRequestContentType string\n\n\t// ExpectedContentType is the expected error message content type reference\n\tExpectedContentType string\n\n\t// SupportedContentTypes are the types that should be mentioned in the error\n\tSupportedContentTypes []string\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE ERROR TEST DATA STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n// These structures provide the fundamental data models for error testing.\n// They are designed to be reusable across different test scenarios and types.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// PatternErrorTestCase represents a single error test case with all necessary validation parameters.\n// This is the core structure that defines what to test and how to validate the response.\ntype PatternErrorTestCase struct {\n\t// Name is the test case name for reporting\n\tName string `json:\"name\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// Description explains what this test case validates\n\tDescription string `json:\"description\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request `json:\"-\"`\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int `json:\"expected_status\" validate:\"required,min=100,max=599\"`\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n\tExpectedCode string `json:\"expected_code\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// ExpectedMessageSubstring is text that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedMessageSubstring string `json:\"expected_message_substring,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ExpectedMessageKeywords are alternative keywords (any match is acceptable)\n\tExpectedMessageKeywords []string `json:\"expected_message_keywords,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n\tMinMessageLength int `json:\"min_message_length,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration `json:\"max_response_time,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RequiresAuth indicates whether the request needs valid authentication\n\tRequiresAuth bool `json:\"requires_auth,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ErrorCategory categorizes the error type\n\tErrorCategory string `json:\"error_category,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// SkipCORSValidation disables CORS header checks\n\tSkipCORSValidation bool `json:\"skip_cors_validation,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ValidateResponse provides custom validation beyond standard checks\n\tValidateResponse func(*testing.T, *httptest.ResponseRecorder) `json:\"-\"`\n}\n\n// ErrorTestPatternDefinition defines a pattern of error tests for a specific category.\n// This allows grouping related test cases and applying common validation rules.\ntype ErrorTestPatternDefinition struct {\n\t// Name is the pattern name for identification\n\tName string `json:\"name\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// Description explains what this pattern tests\n\tDescription string `json:\"description\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// ErrorCategory categorizes the errors in this pattern\n\tErrorCategory string `json:\"error_category\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// TestCases contains the individual test cases for this pattern\n\tTestCases []PatternErrorTestCase `json:\"test_cases\" validate:\"required,min=1\"`\n\n\t// DefaultValidation provides pattern-level validation rules\n\tDefaultValidation ErrorValidationRules `json:\"default_validation,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PatternType indicates the type of error pattern (auth, non-auth, CORS, etc.)\n\tPatternType string `json:\"pattern_type\" validate:\"required,oneof=auth nonauth cors contenttype general\"`\n}\n\n// ErrorValidationRules defines common validation rules for error responses.\n// These rules can be applied at pattern level or overridden per test case.\ntype ErrorValidationRules struct {\n\t// RequireXMLValidation ensures response is valid XML\n\tRequireXMLValidation bool `json:\"require_xml_validation,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// DefaultMinMessageLength sets default minimum message length\n\tDefaultMinMessageLength int `json:\"default_min_message_length,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RequireCORSHeaders ensures CORS headers are present\n\tRequireCORSHeaders bool `json:\"require_cors_headers,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ExpectedContentType sets expected content type\n\tExpectedContentType string `json:\"expected_content_type,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime sets maximum acceptable response time\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration `json:\"max_response_time,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ErrorTestSuiteConfig represents configuration for an error test suite.\n// This provides the top-level structure for organizing comprehensive error testing.\ntype ErrorTestSuiteConfig struct {\n\t// Name is the suite name for identification\n\tName string `json:\"name\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// Description explains what this suite tests\n\tDescription string `json:\"description\" validate:\"required\"`\n\n\t// Patterns contains the test patterns in this suite\n\tPatterns []ErrorTestPatternDefinition `json:\"patterns\" validate:\"required,min=1\"`\n\n\t// GlobalValidation provides suite-level validation rules\n\tGlobalValidation ErrorValidationRules `json:\"global_validation,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Metadata contains additional suite information\n\tMetadata ErrorTestSuiteMetadata `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ErrorTestSuiteMetadata contains metadata about the error test suite.\ntype ErrorTestSuiteMetadata struct {\n\t// Version tracks the suite version\n\tVersion string `json:\"version,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Author identifies who created the suite\n\tAuthor string `json:\"author,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Created indicates when the suite was created\n\tCreated time.Time `json:\"created,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Updated indicates when the suite was last updated\n\tUpdated time.Time `json:\"updated,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Tags provide searchable keywords\n\tTags []string `json:\"tags,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ErrorResponse represents a parsed error response for validation.\ntype ErrorResponse struct {\n\t// StatusCode is the HTTP status code received\n\tStatusCode int `json:\"status_code\"`\n\n\t// ErrorCode is the S3 error code from the response\n\tErrorCode string `json:\"error_code\"`\n\n\t// ErrorMessage is the error message from the response\n\tErrorMessage string `json:\"error_message\"`\n\n\t// ResponseTime is how long the request took\n\tResponseTime time.Duration `json:\"response_time\"`\n\n\t// Headers contains the response headers\n\tHeaders map[string]string `json:\"headers,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Body contains the raw response body\n\tBody string `json:\"body,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// ErrorTestResult represents the result of running a single error test.\ntype ErrorTestResult struct {\n\t// TestCaseName is the name of the test case that was run\n\tTestCaseName string `json:\"test_case_name\"`\n\n\t// PatternName is the name of the pattern this test belongs to\n\tPatternName string `json:\"pattern_name,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Passed indicates whether the test passed\n\tPassed bool `json:\"passed\"`\n\n\t// Response contains the actual error response received\n\tResponse ErrorResponse `json:\"response\"`\n\n\t// FailureReason explains why the test failed (if applicable)\n\tFailureReason string `json:\"failure_reason,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Duration is how long the test took to run\n\tDuration time.Duration `json:\"duration\"`\n\n\t// Timestamp indicates when the test was run\n\tTimestamp time.Time `json:\"timestamp\"`\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// PREDEFINED ERROR TEST TABLES\n// checks\n\tvalidator := VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration)\n\n\t// Always validate HTTP status\n\tvalidator.HTTPStatusCode(tt.ExpectedStatus)\n\n\t// Always validate Content-Type\n\tvalidator.ContentType(\"application/xml\")\n\n\t// Always validate error code\n\tif tt.ExpectedCode != \"\" {\n\t\tvalidator.HasCode(tt.ExpectedCode)\n\t}\n\n\t// Always validate message is present\n\tvalidator.BodyNotEmpty()\n\n\t// Always validate XML declaration\n\tvalidator.HasXMLDeclaration()\n\n\t// Validate message substring or keywords\n\tif tt.ExpectedMessageSubstring != \"\" {\n\t\tvalidator.HasMessage(tt.ExpectedMessageSubstring)\n\t} else if len(tt.ExpectedMessageKeywords) > 0 {\n\t\tvalidator.MessageContainsAny(tt.ExpectedMessageKeywords...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate minimum message length\n\tif tt.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n\t\tvalidator.MessageMinLength(tt.MinMessageLength)\n\t} else {\n\t\tvalidator.MessageMinLength(15) // Default minimum\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate response time if specified\n\tif tt.MaxResponseTime > 0 {\n\t\tvalidator.ResponseTime(tt.MaxResponseTime)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate CORS headers unless skipped\n\tif !tt.SkipCORSValidation {\n\t\tvalidator.HasCORSHeaders()\n\t}\n\n\t// Execute all validations\n\tvalidator.Assert()\n\n\t// Run custom validation if provided\n\tif tt.ValidateResponse != nil {\n\t\ttt.ValidateResponse(t, w)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TABLE-DRIVEN TEST RUNNERS\n// Missing required query parameters\n44\t//   - Invalid query string values\n45\t//   - Malformed request body\n46\t//   - Invalid header values\n47\t//\n48\t// Usage:\n49\t//\n50\t//\tscenario := BadRequestScenario()\n51\t//\tscenario.Name = \"My custom bad request test\"\n52\t//\tscenario.SetupRequest = func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n53\t//\t    return CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key?invalid=param\")\n54\t//\t}\n55\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n56\tfunc BadRequestScenario() ErrorScenario {\n57\t\treturn ErrorScenario{\n58\t\t\tName:        \"Bad Request - Invalid Request\",\n59\t\t\tDescription: \"Request with invalid parameters should return 400 Bad Request\",\n60\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n61\t\t\t\t// Create an authenticated request with invalid query parameters\n62\t\t\t\treturn CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key?invalid=parameter&malformed=value\")\n63\t\t\t},\n64\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   400,\n65\t\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n66\t\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"invalid\", \"request\", \"parameter\"},\n67\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n68\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n69\t\t}\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\t// BadRequestWithMissingContentTypeScenario returns a fixture for 400 Bad Request with missing Content-Type.\n73\t//\n74\t// This scenario tests requests that should have a Content-Type header but don't.\n75\t// Common causes:\n76\t//   - PUT/POST requests without Content-Type\n77\t//   - Missing required content negotiation headers\n78\t//\n79\t// Usage:\n80\t//\n81\t//\tscenario := BadRequestWithMissingContentTypeScenario()\n82\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n83\tfunc BadRequestWithMissingContentTypeScenario() ErrorScenario {\n84\t\treturn ErrorScenario{\n85\t\t\tName:        \"Bad Request - Missing Content-Type\",\n86\t\t\tDescription: \"Request without Content-Type header should return 400\",\n87\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n88\t\t\t\tbody := `{\"data\": \"test\"}`\n89\t\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"PUT\", \"/test-bucket/key\", body, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n90\t\t\t},\n91\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   400,\n92\t\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n93\t\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"content\", \"type\", \"required\"},\n94\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n95\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n96\t\t}\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t// BadRequestWithInvalidQueryScenario returns a fixture for 400 Bad Request with invalid query string.\n100\t//\n101\t// This scenario tests requests with malformed or unsupported query parameters.\n102\t// Common causes:\n103\t//   - Invalid query parameter names\n104\t//   - Invalid query parameter values\n105\t//   - Conflicting query parameters\n106\t//\n107\t// Usage:\n108\t//\n109\t//\tscenario := BadRequestWithInvalidQueryScenario()\n110\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n111\tfunc BadRequestWithInvalidQueryScenario() ErrorScenario {\n112\t\treturn ErrorScenario{\n113\t\t\tName:        \"Bad Request - Invalid Query Parameters\",\n114\t\t\tDescription: \"Request with invalid query parameters should return 400\",\n115\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n116\t\t\t\treturn CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key?unsupported-parameter=value&conflicting=param\")\n117\t\t\t},\n118\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   400,\n119\t\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n120\t\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"invalid\", \"query\", \"parameter\"},\n121\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n122\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n123\t\t}\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\t// NotFoundScenario returns a fixture for 404 Not Found with resource error.\n127\t//\n128\t// This scenario tests requests for non-existent resources.\n129\t// Common causes:\n130\t//   - Object was deleted\n131\t//   - Wrong bucket name\n132\t//   - Wrong key name\n133\t//   - Typo in resource path\n134\t//\n135\t// Usage:\n136\t//\n137\t//\tscenario := NotFoundScenario()\n138\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n139\tfunc NotFoundScenario() ErrorScenario {\n140\t\treturn ErrorScenario{\n141\t\t\tName:        \"Not Found - Non-existent Resource\",\n142\t\t\tDescription: \"Request for non-existent resource should return 404\",\n143\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n144\t\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent-key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n145\t\t\t},\n146\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   404,\n147\t\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n148\t\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\"},\n149\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n150\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n151\t\t}\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\t// NotFoundWithBucketScenario returns a fixture for 404 with non-existent bucket.\n155\t//\n156\t// This scenario tests requests for a bucket that doesn't exist.\n157\t//\n158\t// Usage:\n159\t//\n160\t//\tscenario := NotFoundWithBucketScenario(\"nonexistent-bucket\")\n161\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n162\tfunc NotFoundWithBucketScenario(bucket string) ErrorScenario {\n163\t\treturn ErrorScenario{\n164\t\t\tName:        \"Not Found - Non-existent Bucket\",\n165\t\t\tDescription: \"Request for non-existent bucket should return 404\",\n166\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n167\t\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/\"+bucket+\"/key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n168\t\t\t},\n169\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   404,\n170\t\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n171\t\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"bucket\", \"not\", \"found\"},\n172\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n173\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n174\t\t}\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t// InternalServerErrorScenario returns a fixture for 500 Internal Server Error.\n178\t//\n179\t// This scenario tests unexpected server-side errors.\n180\t// Common causes:\n181\t//   - Server-side bug\n182\t//   - Infrastructure failure\n183\t//   - Database connection failure\n184\t//   - Unexpected error condition\n185\t//\n186\t// NOTE: This scenario requires special server setup or mocking to trigger a genuine\n187\t// 500 error. In production, ARMOR is designed to handle errors gracefully and return\n188\t// appropriate 4xx errors. To test 500 errors, you may need to:\n189\t// 1. Modify the server to inject errors for testing\n190\t// 2. Use a mock server that returns 500 errors\n191\t// 3. Test against a server with induced failures (e.g., disable B2 backend)\n192\t//\n193\t// Usage:\n194\t//\n195\t//\tscenario := InternalServerErrorScenario()\n196\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n197\t//\t// If result.Passed is false, check if the server is configured to return 500\n198\tfunc InternalServerErrorScenario() ErrorScenario {\n199\t\treturn ErrorScenario{\n200\t\t\tName:        \"Internal Server Error\",\n201\t\t\tDescription: \"Server error should return 500 with error details (requires special setup)\",\n202\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n203\t\t\t\t// This creates a request that might trigger an internal error\n204\t\t\t\t// NOTE: In the current ARMOR implementation, this path returns 403\n205\t\t\t\t// To properly test 500 errors, you need to:\n206\t\t\t\t// 1. Use a mock server, or\n207\t\t\t\t// 2. Disable the B2 backend to trigger connection errors, or\n208\t\t\t\t// 3. Add server-side error injection for testing\n209\t\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key-that-does-not-exist\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n210\t\t\t},\n211\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   500,\n212\t\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInternalError,\n213\t\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"internal\", \"error\"},\n214\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n215\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n216\t\t}\n217\t}\n218\t\n219\t// =============================================================================\n220\t// SCENARIO EXECUTION HELPERS\n221\t// =============================================================================\n222\t\n223\t// runScenario executes a single error scenario and validates the outcome.\n224\t//\n225\t// This helper:\n226\t// 1. Creates the request using the scenario's SetupRequest function\n227\t// 2. Executes the request against the test server fixture\n228\t// 3. Measures response time\n229\t// 4. Validates the response against the scenario's expectations\n230\t// 5. Returns a result object with the outcome\n231\t//\n232\t// Parameters:\n233\t//   - t: Testing instance for context and assertions\n234\t//   - fixture: Test server fixture to execute the request against\n235\t//   - scenario: Error scenario to execute and validate\n236\t//\n237\t// Returns: ErrorScenarioResult with the execution details and validation outcome\n238\t//\n239\t// Example:\n240\t//\n241\t//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n242\t//\tif !result.Passed {\n243\t//\t    t.Logf(\"Scenario failed: %s - %s\", result.Name, result.ErrorMessage)\n244\t//\t}\n245\tfunc runScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n246\t\tt.Helper()\n247\t\n248\t\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n249\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n250\t\n251\t\t// Measure response time\n252\t\tstart := time.Now()\n253\t\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n254\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n255\t\n256\t\t// Parse response\n257\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n258\t\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n259\t\n260\t\t// Build result\n261\t\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n262\t\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n263\t\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n264\t\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n265\t\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n266\t\t\tDuration:     duration,\n267\t\t}\n268\t\n269\t\t// Validate result\n270\t\tresult.Passed = true\n271\t\n272\t\t// Validate HTTP status\n273\t\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n274\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n275\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n276\t\t}\n277\t\n278\t\t// Validate error code\n279\t\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n280\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n281\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n282\t\t}\n283\t\n284\t\t// Validate message length\n285\t\tminLength := scenario.MinMessageLength\n286\t\tif minLength == 0 {\n287\t\t\tminLength = 15 // Default minimum\n288\t\t}\n289\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n290\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n291\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, len(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n292\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n293\t\t}\n294\t\n295\t\t// Validate keywords if specified\n296\t\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n297\t\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n298\t\t\tfound := false\n299\t\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n300\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n301\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n302\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n303\t\t\t\t}\n304\t\t\t}\n305\t\t\tif !found {\n306\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n307\t\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedKeywords, s3Err.Message)\n308\t\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n309\t\t\t}\n310\t\t}\n311\t\n312\t\t// Validate response time if specified\n313\t\tif scenario.MaxResponseTime > 0 && duration > scenario.MaxResponseTime {\n314\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", scenario.Name, duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n315\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Validate Content-Type header\n319\t\texpectedContentType := \"application/xml\"\n320\t\tactualContentType := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n321\t\tif actualContentType != expectedContentType {\n322\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, expectedContentType, actualContentType)\n323\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n324\t\t}\n325\t\n326\t\treturn result\n327\t}\n328\t\n329\t// =============================================================================\n330\t// TABLE-DRIVEN TEST EXAMPLES\n331\t// =============================================================================\n332\t// The following examples demonstrate how to use the fixtures in table-driven tests.\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t\n335\t/*\n336\tExample 1: Basic error scenario table\n337\t\n338\t\tfunc TestBasicErrorScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n339\t\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n340\t\n341\t\t    scenarios := []ErrorScenario{\n342\t\t        BadRequestScenario(),\n343\t\t        NotFoundScenario(),\n344\t\t        InternalServerErrorScenario(),\n345\t\t    }\n346\t\n347\t\t    for _, scenario := range scenarios {\n348\t\t        t.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n349\t\t            result := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n350\t\t            if !result.Passed {\n351\t\t                t.Errorf(\"Scenario failed: %s\", result.Message)\n352\t\t            }\n353\t\t        })\n354\t\t    }\n355\t\t}\n356\t*/\n357\t\n358\t/*\n359\tExample 2: Customized scenarios based on fixtures\n360\t\n361\t\tfunc TestCustomBadRequestScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n362\t\t    fixture := ExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"key\", \"invalid\"},\n385\t\t            MinMessageLength:  15,\n386\t\t        },\n387\t\t    }\n388\t\n389\t\t    for _, scenario := range scenarios {\n390\t\t        t.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n391\t\t            result := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n392\t\t            if !result.Passed {\n393\t\t                t.Errorf(\"Scenario failed: TestExtendedNotFoundScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n404\t\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n405\t\n406\t\t    scenarios := []ErrorScenario{\n407\t\t        func() ErrorScenario {\n408\t\t          scenario.Name = \"Non-existent bucket with performance check\"\n416\t\t            scenario.MaxResponseTime = 50 * time.Millisecond\n417\t\t            return scenario\n418\t\t server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SCENARIO FIXTURES\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides reusable test fixtures for common error response scenarios.\n// These fixtures enable rapid test authoring for new error types.\n//\n// Usage:\n//   1. Use ErrorScenario struct to define test scenarios\n//   2. Use predefined fixtures (400, 404, 500) for common cases\n//   3. Use runScenario() helper to execute and validate\n//   4. See table-driven examples at the bottom of this file\n// =============================================================================\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SCENARIO STRUCT\n// =============================================================================\n// The ErrorScenario struct is defined in error_test_infrastructure_test.go\n// This file provides fixture functions that return ErrorScenario instances\n// =============================================================================\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMMON ERROR SCENARIO FIXTURES\n// =============================================================================\n// These fixtures represent the most common error scenarios and can be used\n// directly in tests or as templates for custom scenarios.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// BadRequestScenario returns a fixture for 400 Bad Request with validation errors.\n//\n// This scenario tests requests that are malformed or missing required parameters.\n// Common causes:\n//   - Missing required query parameters\n//   - Invalid query string values\n//   - Malformed request body\n//   - Invalid header values\n//\n// Usage:\n//\n//\tscenario := BadRequestScenario()\n//\tscenario.Name = \"My custom bad request test\"\n//\tscenario.SetupRequest = func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n//\t    return CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key?invalid=param\")\n//\t}\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\nfunc BadRequestScenario() ErrorScenario {\n\treturn ErrorScenario{\n\t\tName:        \"Bad Request - Invalid Request\",\n\t\tDescription: \"Request with invalid parameters should return 400 Bad Request\",\n\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n\t\t\t// Create an authenticated request with invalid query parameters\n\t\t\treturn CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key?invalid=parameter&malformed=value\")\n\t\t},\n\t\tExpectedStatus:   400,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"invalid\", \"request\", \"parameter\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n\t}\n}\n\n// BadRequestWithMissingContentTypeScenario returns a fixture for 400 Bad Request with missing Content-Type.\n//\n// This scenario tests requests that should have a Content-Type header but don't.\n// Common causes:\n//   - PUT/POST requests without Content-Type\n//   - Missing required content negotiation headers\n//\n// Usage:\n//\n//\tscenario := BadRequestWithMissingContentTypeScenario()\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\nfunc BadRequestWithMissingContentTypeScenario() ErrorScenario {\n\treturn ErrorScenario{\n\t\tName:        \"Bad Request - Missing Content-Type\",\n\t\tDescription: \"Request without Content-Type header should return 400\",\n\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n\t\t\tbody := `{\"data\": \"test\"}`\n\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"PUT\", \"/test-bucket/key\", body, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n\t\t},\n\t\tExpectedStatus:   400,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"content\", \"type\", \"required\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n\t}\n}\n\n// BadRequestWithInvalidQueryScenario returns a fixture for 400 Bad Request with invalid query string.\n//\n// This scenario tests requests with malformed or unsupported query parameters.\n// Common causes:\n//   - Invalid query parameter names\n//   - Invalid query parameter values\n//   - Conflicting query parameters\n//\n// Usage:\n//\n//\tscenario := BadRequestWithInvalidQueryScenario()\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\nfunc BadRequestWithInvalidQueryScenario() ErrorScenario {\n\treturn ErrorScenario{\n\t\tName:        \"Bad Request - Invalid Query Parameters\",\n\t\tDescription: \"Request with invalid query parameters should return 400\",\n\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n\t\t\treturn CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key?unsupported-parameter=value&conflicting=param\")\n\t\t},\n\t\tExpectedStatus:   400,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"invalid\", \"query\", \"parameter\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NotFoundScenario returns a fixture for 404 Not Found with resource error.\n//\n// This scenario tests requests for non-existent resources.\n// Common causes:\n//   - Object was deleted\n//   - Wrong bucket name\n//   - Wrong key name\n//   - Typo in resource path\n//\n// Usage:\n//\n//\tscenario := NotFoundScenario()\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\nfunc NotFoundScenario() ErrorScenario {\n\treturn ErrorScenario{\n\t\tName:        \"Not Found - Non-existent Resource\",\n\t\tDescription: \"Request for non-existent resource should return 404\",\n\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent-key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n\t\t},\n\t\tExpectedStatus:   404,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NotFoundWithBucketScenario returns a fixture for 404 with non-existent bucket.\n//\n// This scenario tests requests for a bucket that doesn't exist.\n//\n// Usage:\n//\n//\tscenario := NotFoundWithBucketScenario(\"nonexistent-bucket\")\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\nfunc NotFoundWithBucketScenario(bucket string) ErrorScenario {\n\treturn ErrorScenario{\n\t\tName:        \"Not Found - Non-existent Bucket\",\n\t\tDescription: \"Request for non-existent bucket should return 404\",\n\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/\"+bucket+\"/key\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n\t\t},\n\t\tExpectedStatus:   404,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"bucket\", \"not\", \"found\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n\t}\n}\n\n// InternalServerErrorScenario returns a fixture for 500 Internal Server Error.\n//\n// This scenario tests unexpected server-side errors.\n// Common causes:\n//   - Server-side bug\n//   - Infrastructure failure\n//   - Database connection failure\n//   - Unexpected error condition\n//\n// NOTE: This scenario requires special server setup or mocking to trigger a genuine\n// 500 error. In production, ARMOR is designed to handle errors gracefully and return\n// appropriate 4xx errors. To test 500 errors, you may need to:\n// 1. Modify the server to inject errors for testing\n// 2. Use a mock server that returns 500 errors\n// 3. Test against a server with induced failures (e.g., disable B2 backend)\n//\n// Usage:\n//\n//\tscenario := InternalServerErrorScenario()\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n//\t// If result.Passed is false, check if the server is configured to return 500\nfunc InternalServerErrorScenario() ErrorScenario {\n\treturn ErrorScenario{\n\t\tName:        \"Internal Server Error\",\n\t\tDescription: \"Server error should return 500 with error details (requires special setup)\",\n\t\tSetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n\t\t\t// This creates a request that might trigger an internal error\n\t\t\t// NOTE: In the current ARMOR implementation, this path returns 403\n\t\t\t// To properly test 500 errors, you need to:\n\t\t\t// 1. Use a mock server, or\n\t\t\t// 2. Disable the B2 backend to trigger connection errors, or\n\t\t\t// 3. Add server-side error injection for testing\n\t\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key-that-does-not-exist\", \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n\t\t},\n\t\tExpectedStatus:   500,\n\t\tExpectedCode:     ErrorCodeInternalError,\n\t\tExpectedKeywords: []string{\"internal\", \"error\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:  100 * time.Millisecond,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SCENARIO EXECUTION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// runScenario executes a single error scenario and validates the outcome.\n//\n// This helper:\n// 1. Creates the request using the scenario's SetupRequest function\n// 2. Executes the request against the test server fixture\n// 3. Measures response time\n// 4. Validates the response against the scenario's expectations\n// 5. Returns a result object with the outcome\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - t: Testing instance for context and assertions\n//   - fixture: Test server fixture to execute the request against\n//   - scenario: Error scenario to execute and validate\n//\n// Returns: ErrorScenarioResult with the execution details and validation outcome\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresult := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n//\tif !result.Passed {\n//\t    t.Logf(\"Scenario failed: %s - %s\", result.Name, result.ErrorMessage)\n//\t}\nfunc runScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t// Measure response time\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\n\t// Parse response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n\n\t// Build result\n\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n\t\tDuration:     duration,\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate result\n\tresult.Passed = true\n\n\t// Validate HTTP status\n\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error code\n\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate message length\n\tminLength := scenario.MinMessageLength\n\tif minLength == 0 {\n\t\tminLength = 15 // Default minimum\n\t}\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tscenario.Name, len(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate keywords if specified\n\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedKeywords, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate response time if specified\n\tif scenario.MaxResponseTime > 0 && duration > scenario.MaxResponseTime {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", scenario.Name, duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate Content-Type header\n\texpectedContentType := \"application/xml\"\n\tactualContentType := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif actualContentType != expectedContentType {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, expectedContentType, actualContentType)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TABLE-DRIVEN TEST EXAMPLES\n// =============================================================================\n// The following examples demonstrate how to use the fixtures in table-driven tests.\n// =============================================================================\n\n/*\nExample 1: Basic error scenario table\n\n\tfunc TestBasicErrorScenarios(t *testing.T) {\n\t    fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n\t    scenarios := []ErrorScenario{\n\t        BadRequestScenario(),\n\t        NotFoundScenario(),\n\t        InternalServerErrorScenario(),\n\t    }\n\n\t    for _, scenario := range scenarios {\n\t        t.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t            result := runScenario(t, fixture, scenario)\n\t            if !result.Passed {\n\t                t.Errorf(\"Scenario failed: %s\", result.Message)\n\t            }\n\t        })\n\t    }\n\t}\n*/\n\n/*\nExample 2: Customized scenarios based on fixtures\n\n\tfunc TestCustomBadRequestScenarios(t
